title: [8] Package MCP server for public distribution — GoReleaser multi-platform release + go install
why_we_need_this: `wireguard-vpn` is a public, soon-open-sourced project and external users need to run the MCP server (`mcp/`) against their OWN WireGuard deployments. Today the only documented install (mcp/README.md, Phase 5 / task #7) is hand-building the binary from a repo subdirectory — a poor, unreproducible, and untrustworthy UX for public consumers of a SECURITY-SENSITIVE tool that can add and delete VPN peers. Phase 5 documented the build command, host-config, and cross-project reuse, but NOT a distribution mechanism. This task closes that gap: versioned, cross-platform, checksummed (and ideally signed) release artifacts via GoReleaser, plus a first-class `go install` path, so external users get supply-chain-safe, reproducible installs without hand-compiling. Homebrew is explicitly deferred (owner decision 2026-07-07: `go install` + release binaries only). Each public user retargets the same generic binary to their own tunnel via the existing `MCP_DASHBOARD_ADDR`/`-addr` override (the Phase 5 "one server per project" template).
acceptance_criteria:
- Add a GoReleaser config (`.goreleaser.yaml`) scoped to the `mcp/` Go module that builds the `mcp-server` binary for all mainstream targets: `linux/amd64`, `linux/arm64`, `darwin/amd64`, `darwin/arm64`, `windows/amd64` (windows/arm64 optional). Single static binary, `CGO_ENABLED=0`, `-trimpath`, and ldflags stamping version/commit — consistent with the repo's single-static-binary philosophy. The binary output name stays `wireguard-mcp` (matches mcp/README.md and go.mod module).
- GoReleaser must build from the `mcp/` SUBDIRECTORY correctly — `mcp/` is a separate Go module (`wireguard-mcp`) inside a Terraform monorepo. Set the right `builds[].dir`/`main`/`project_name`; verify the module-subdir build actually resolves.
- Produce per-platform archives PLUS a `checksums.txt` (SHA256). Sign the checksums/artifacts via cosign keyless (GitHub OIDC) and/or emit SLSA build provenance — this is a peer-mutating VPN tool, so supply-chain attestation is baseline, not gold-plating. If signing must be deferred for a first cut, say so explicitly IN THE DELIVERABLE and leave a scoped follow-up rather than dropping it silently.
- Add a GitHub Actions release workflow (`.github/workflows/`) that runs GoReleaser on tag push and publishes a GitHub Release with the artifacts. Wire tokens correctly: `GITHUB_TOKEN` for the release. Decide and DOCUMENT the tag scheme — because `mcp/` is a submodule of a Terraform monorepo, prefer `mcp/vX.Y.Z`-style tags (or justify plain `vX.Y.Z`) so MCP releases don't collide with any future repo/infra versioning; make the workflow trigger match the chosen scheme.
- `go install` is the PRIMARY documented install path: exact version-pinned command (`go install github.com/vkatrichenko/wireguard-vpn/mcp/cmd/mcp-server@<tag>`), the resulting binary name, and how it lands on `$GOBIN`/`$PATH`. Confirm the module path resolves for `go install` given the monorepo layout (the module is at `mcp/`, package at `mcp/cmd/mcp-server`).
- Update `mcp/README.md` with an install/distribution section covering BOTH paths — (a) `go install`, (b) download a checksummed release binary + verify — and refreshed `mcpServers` config examples for each (installed-by-name vs absolute-path), each retaining the `MCP_DASHBOARD_ADDR`/`-addr` override so each user points at their own tunnel. Note macOS Gatekeeper quarantine on downloaded binaries and the `xattr -d com.apple.quarantine` workaround (since Homebrew — which would auto-strip it — is deferred).
- Verify locally with `goreleaser release --snapshot --clean` (or `goreleaser build --snapshot --clean`) that the full OS/arch matrix builds and archives/checksums are produced — do NOT publish. Capture the snapshot output (target list + checksum file) in the final report.
- Homebrew is OUT OF SCOPE for this task (deferred). Do not add a `brews:` block or a tap; a one-line note that GoReleaser's `brews:` can be bolted on later is fine.
important_constraints:
- Public distribution + supply-chain safety is the whole point — checksums are mandatory; signing (cosign/OIDC or SLSA provenance) strongly preferred for a tool that can mutate VPN peers. Deferring signing requires an explicit written flag + follow-up, not silent omission.
- Homebrew explicitly deferred by owner (2026-07-07): `go install` + GoReleaser release binaries only. Do not implement a tap.
- FIRST CI IN THE REPO: this adds a GitHub Actions release workflow. The CLAUDE.md "no CI/CD" rule is specifically about not auto-running `terraform apply` / infra CI — it does NOT forbid a release-artifact workflow. Still, adding CI is a real decision: call it out prominently and get explicit owner sign-off before merging the workflow (leave it clearly separable in the PR if approval is pending).
- Packaging/release ONLY. Do NOT change any MCP tool code, tool behavior, or the confirmation-gate design; do NOT add auth; do NOT introduce Docker or an always-on listener. Do NOT modify `dashboard/` or `terraform/`.
- New/changed files limited to: `.goreleaser.yaml`, a `.github/workflows/` release file, and `mcp/README.md` (+ possibly a `.gitignore` entry for `dist/`). Do NOT edit `project-context/` directly (Praxis-managed — handled via `update_project_context` after).
- `mcp/` is a separate Go module inside a Terraform monorepo — GoReleaser and `go install` must both cope with the subdir module path; validate, don't assume.
- Follow repo git conventions: `feat:`-prefixed commit (adds build/release tooling), `feat/`-prefixed branch, PR-based.
- After the work lands, run `update_project_context` so the mcp-server route records the distribution mechanism (GoReleaser release + `go install`, Homebrew deferred) and, if the release workflow is added, that the repo now has release CI (distinct from the still-absent infra-apply CI).
